violations.text: Various deli cheeses was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

Cooked Sausages was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food items were moved into walk-in cooler during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

Salmon filets were observed thawing within intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP) in the walk-in cooler. Seafood items packaged within ROP packaging shall be completely removed from their original packaging prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen into the food item. Food items were observed still partially frozen and were removed from their original packaging during the time of inspection. Guidance document provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live activity on glue monitor traps. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is work with pest control to develop an action plan to address pest activity. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on both cookline prep units. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

The drain plugs for the kitchen 3-bay sink were observed to be missing, and paper towels were observed to plugging drains. PIC order drain plugs during the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service was called time of inspection. Until unit is properly functioning establishment is sanitize dishes and utensils in the sanitizing bay of the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The cutting boards on the cookline prep units and the prep table cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2024

Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on prep unit #1, the 2-door low boy, and the 1-door reach-in. Refrigerator door gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024
The door to the kitchen toilet room is not self-closing. Toilet rooms that open to food preparation areas shall be self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2024
